spec:
SPI Master controller, Mode 0 (CPOL=0, CPHA=0), 8-bit, MSB-first. Parameter CLKS_PER_HALF_BIT controls SPI clock speed (default 2, so sclk = clk/4).

Inputs:
- clk: system clock
- rst_n: active-low asynchronous reset
- tx_start: pulse high 1 cycle to begin transaction
- tx_data[7:0]: byte to transmit (latched on tx_start)
- miso: master-in slave-out

Outputs:
- rx_data[7:0]: received byte (valid when rx_valid pulses)
- rx_valid: pulses high 1 cycle when rx_data is complete
- tx_ready: high when idle and ready for new tx_start
- sclk: SPI clock (idle low in Mode 0)
- mosi: master-out slave-in (MSB first)

SPI Mode 0: sclk idles low, data sampled on rising edge, shifted on falling edge. MSB transmitted first.

Behavior:
- Reset: sclk=0, mosi=0, tx_ready=1, rx_valid=0
- tx_start: latches tx_data, drives bit 7 on MOSI, generates 8 sclk pulses
- On each rising sclk: MISO is sampled into rx_shift
- On each falling sclk: next MOSI bit driven
- After 8 bits: rx_valid pulses, rx_data holds received byte, tx_ready goes high
- tx_start ignored while tx_ready is low

Verify: reset state, MOSI outputs MSB first, MISO sampled on rising edge, 8 sclk pulses per byte, rx_valid pulses once, tx_ready handshake, back-to-back transactions.
